title: Franchise Agreement
why_it_matters: Primary contract governing the franchise relationship. Contains all operational, financial, and legal obligations.
key_burden_or_function: 78-page agreement covering grant, fees, term/renewal, site selection, marks, trade secrets, training, operations manual, advertising, reporting, standards of operation, insurance, termination, post-term obligations, transfer, noncompete, dispute resolution. Includes Schedule 1 (Summary Info), Schedule 2 (Ownership), Schedule 3 (Lease Rider), Schedule 4 (Internet/Social Media/Telephone Assignment).

title: Multi-State Addenda to Franchise Agreement, MUDA, and Disclosure Document
why_it_matters: Modifies franchise agreement and FDD for specific state compliance. Illinois addendum includes fee deferral requirement due to financial condition — a significant diligence signal.
key_burden_or_function: Illinois: fee deferral until pre-opening obligations met and operations commenced (imposed by Attorney General due to financial condition); Illinois law governs; venue restriction void. Minnesota: 90-day termination notice (60 days to cure), 180-day non-renewal notice; trademark protection obligation; release limitations; jury trial waiver deleted.

title: Multi-Unit Development Agreement (MUDA)
why_it_matters: Governs multi-unit development rights for 2–3 unit franchisees. Defines development schedule and default/termination provisions.
key_burden_or_function: Grants right to develop additional units per schedule. 2nd FA within 12 months of 1st opening; 3rd FA within 24 months of 2nd opening. Termination without cure if development schedule not met or any FA default. Franchisee's liability limited (option nature). Conditions: financial/organizational capacity and full brand compliance. NC law governs. Includes Ohio cancellation notice.

title: List of State Administrators
why_it_matters: Standard FDD exhibit listing state franchise regulators.
key_burden_or_function: Lists administrators for CA, HI, IL, IN, MD, MI, MN, NY, ND, OR, RI, SD, VA, WA, WI.

title: List of State Agents for Service of Process
why_it_matters: Standard FDD exhibit. Lists appointed agents in registration states.
key_burden_or_function: Agents appointed in CA, HI, IL, IN, MD, MI, MN, NY, ND, RI, SD, VA, WA, WI.

title: Unlimited Guaranty and Assumption of Obligations Agreement
why_it_matters: Creates unlimited personal liability for guarantors. Jointly and severally bound by all FA terms including noncompete, non-solicitation, dispute resolution, and indemnification.
key_burden_or_function: Unconditional guaranty of full performance and all payments. Guarantor personally bound by all FA terms. Liability not contingent on pursuit of remedies against franchisee. Not diminished by insolvency, bankruptcy, or amendment. Irrevocable. Effective for full FA term including renewals. NC law governs.

title: General Release (Sample)
why_it_matters: Required upon transfer or successor franchise. Mutual release form. Standard but broad.
key_burden_or_function: Mutual release of all claims known and unknown, from beginning of time to signature date. Non-disparagement covenant. Not for execution with initial FA — only on transfer or renewal. Subject to state law limitations (IL, MN addenda restrict release requirements).

title: Nondisclosure and Non-Competition Agreement
why_it_matters: Restricts key personnel from competing during and after relationship. Defines Competitive Business broadly as any childcare, daycare, or preschool services.
key_burden_or_function: During relationship: no competitive business anywhere. Post-termination: 2 years within 10-mile radius of location/territory (whichever greater) and 10 miles of any other Ivybrook location. Covers officers, directors, employees, consultants, managers, operators, independent contractors. NC law governs. Charlotte, NC venue. Company is third-party beneficiary.

title: Confidential Brand Standards Manual — Table of Contents
why_it_matters: Shows scope of operational standards manual. 242 total pages across 6 sections.
key_burden_or_function: Sections: Preface & Introduction (32 pp), Establishing My Franchise Business (76 pp), Personnel (77 pp), Administrative Procedures (13 pp), Daily Procedures (17 pp), Selling & Marketing (27 pp). Total: 242 pages.

title: Financial Statements
why_it_matters: Audited financial statements for FY2022, FY2023, and FY2024. Critical for assessing franchisor financial health — especially given Special Risk #2 flagging financial condition concerns. However, all 30 pages are scanned images with no extractable text.
key_burden_or_function: Should contain balance sheets, income statements, cash flow statements, notes to financial statements, and auditor's report for three fiscal years ending December 31. Content cannot be extracted via text tools — image-only pages.

title: Current and Former Franchisees + Franchise Disclosure Questionnaire
why_it_matters: Lists all current franchisees (as of Jan 1, 2025), not-yet-opened franchisees, and former franchisees. Essential for diligence calls. Also contains standard FDD questionnaire.
key_burden_or_function: Open schools: ~35 franchisee entries across AR, CO, FL, GA, KY, NE, NC, OH, PA, SC, TN, TX, VA. Multi-unit operators marked with asterisk. Not-yet-opened: ~42 entries across ~22 states. Former franchisees: 8 entries including mutual terminations, terminations before opening, and system departures. 3 franchisees ceased communication. Questionnaire is standard pre-execution compliance certification.

title: Receipt (Franchisor and Franchisee copies)
why_it_matters: Standard FDD receipt pages. Lists issuance date, franchise brokers/sellers, and receipt timing requirements by state.
key_burden_or_function: Two copies of receipt for signature. States specific timing requirements: 14 calendar days (standard), 10 business days (NY, MI), earlier of first meeting or 14 days (IA).

title: Lease Rider
why_it_matters: Required rider to franchisee's lease. Gives franchisor significant rights over the premises including option to lease on termination.
key_burden_or_function: Permitted use restricted to franchised preschool. 25% non-compete clause for same shopping center. Franchisor's standard signage rights. No landlord consent needed for assignment to franchisor/affiliates. Franchisor gets notice and cure rights matching franchisee's cure period. Option to lease premises on termination/non-renewal/expiration. De-identification obligations. Franchisor is intended third-party beneficiary. Non-disturbance requirement from mortgage lenders.

title: Internet, Social Media, and Telephone Assignment
why_it_matters: Assigns all phone numbers, email addresses, online presences, and social media accounts to franchisor on termination/expiration. Includes irrevocable power of attorney.
key_burden_or_function: On termination/expiration: all telephone numbers (including personal cell numbers used with business), directory listings, email addresses using Marks, Online Presences, and all content assigned to franchisor. Irrevocable power of attorney for 2 years post-termination. Cannot be revoked.
